title: Problem-Driven Design Patterns · Program Defense
summary: Add pricing, notifications, imports, commands, and persistence without turning patterns into ceremony. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for an extensible library operations toolkit. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use strategy objects or callables when an algorithm varies independently and must be selected or tested explicitly.
Use factory functions or objects when construction choice, configuration, validation, or lifecycle must be centralized.
Wrap an incompatible external API behind the small role the application actually needs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
